sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
MOUSE on the bottom green square (N) changed the board. Looking at the image, I can see the first bottom square now has a white border around it - it's been selected! The bottom row still shows [N, p, b, Y] but the first one is now highlighted with a white frame.
So MOUSE on bottom squares selects them. Now I need to understand what happens next. Let me try clicking on a different bottom square to see if it switches selection, or try SPACE while a square is selected to see if that does something different.
Let me also check the segmentation more carefully to see if there's a new white border object around the selected square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# MOUSE on bottom green square selected it (white border appeared)
# Let me check the full segmentation to see the selection indicator
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes with small pixel counts:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 30:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Now let me try SPACE while a square is selected to see if it does something different
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nSPACE while selected: {last_action_result}")

# Check bottom row after
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("Bottom row after SPACE with selection:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if 54 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 63 and n['pixels'] <= 30:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
